instructions: Public repository: NEVER commit secrets, API keys, personal data, or confidential/proprietary content. Before every commit: inspect `git diff --cached` for patterns matching (API|SECRET|KEY|TOKEN|PASSWORD|PASSWD|CREDENTIALS|sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]). If any match is found in staged files, DO NOT COMMIT — remove the file or replace the value with a placeholder first.
Before any implementation work and before any git operation (commit, branch, push, PR): Read BACKLOG.md, CHANGELOG.md, and VERSION.

Work discipline: Before writing any code or editing any production file, verify (1) the task is listed as an active item in BACKLOG.md under "In Progress" or in a section explicitly marked as current phase, and (2) the current git branch name matches that backlog item or its parent phase. If no backlog item matches, do NOT start work — ask the user to create one. If the branch doesn't match, switch or create the correct branch first. This prevents unplanned work and branch drift.
On task completion: Read BACKLOG.md and CHANGELOG.md to verify tracking is updated.

Before implementation: Read .design/SPEC.md to discover which feature tag, design docs, and functional specs apply to the feature. Read those design docs and specs before writing any code. After implementation: Update .design/ if visual/UX decisions changed; update .specs/ if requirements or data structures changed. Never modify .design/ or .specs/ without user confirmation for proposed changes.

GitHub Issues and Projects: ALL operations are scoped exclusively to noexcuse-no/www-public. Never read or write Issues/Projects in any other repository. Always ask the user for explicit confirmation before any write operation (create, update, close, comment, reassign, modify project fields). Default to read-only. The contextual rule file .omo/rules/github-issues-projects.md has full details.

Todo management: Never create or modify todo lists during planning, research, discussion, or when waiting for user confirmation. Only create todos after the user has explicitly confirmed that implementation should start.
